message.content: {"items":[{"title":"European Working Conditions Survey 2010","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11794","justification":"The EWCS 2010 covers working conditions and various aspects of occupational health and safety for workers across many European countries including Belgium, Denmark, Germany, Greece, Spain, Finland, France, Ireland, Italy, Luxembourg, Netherlands, Austria, Portugal, Sweden, United Kingdom, Bulgaria, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Estonia, Hungary, Latvia, Lithuania, Malta, Poland, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Turkey, Croatia, Switzerland, Norway, and others. Fieldwork took place in 2010, directly within the 2010s."},{"title":"European Working Conditions Survey 2015","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12908","justification":"The 2015 wave of the EWCS provides detailed microdata on working conditions, work organisation, and occupational health indicators for a wide set of European countries including nearly all EU member states and associated countries listed in the query. The survey was conducted in 2015, squarely within the 2010s."},{"title":"European Working Conditions Survey 2015 (Fact Sheets)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12953","justification":"This dataset contains country fact sheets derived from the 2015 EWCS, summarising working conditions and occupational health information by country (including many of the specified European countries). It is explicitly focused on working conditions and is dated to 2015 (2010s)."},{"title":"European Company Survey 2013","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12064","justification":"The European Company Survey 2013 examines workplace practices, work organisation, and human resource management in establishments across European countries including many listed (e.g. EU member states plus some candidate countries). It includes indicators on working conditions and aspects affecting occupational health. The reference year 2013 lies in the 2010s."},{"title":"European Company Survey 2019","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13542","justification":"Although fieldwork is at the end of the decade, ECS 2019 still falls within the 2010s and provides establishment-level data on work organisation, employee participation, and working conditions that influence occupational health. Coverage includes many EU and associated countries (such as those requested)."},{"title":"European Survey of Enterprises on New and Emerging Risks (ESENER-2) 2014","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12532","justification":"ESENER-2 is focused explicitly on occupational health and safety risks in workplaces across European countries, collecting information from establishments on how they manage safety and health at work. The 2014 wave covers many EU states and associated countries and lies within the 2010s."},{"title":"European Survey of Enterprises on New and Emerging Risks (ESENER-3) 2019","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13543","justification":"ESENER-3 (2019) continues the focus on occupational health and safety management and new or emerging work-related risks at establishments across Europe, including many of the countries listed (EU members and others). The survey year 2019 is in the 2010s, and the topic directly concerns occupational health."},{"title":"EUROPEAN SOCIAL SURVEY 2010 (Round 5)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10188","justification":"ESS Round 5 (2010) includes several modules and core items on employment, working conditions, and aspects of job-related well-being that relate to occupational health. It covers many of the European countries in the query (most EU members and some associated countries) and is implemented in 2010, within the 2010s."},{"title":"EUROPEAN SOCIAL SURVEY 2012 (Round 6)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11897","justification":"ESS Round 6 (2012) provides cross-national data including labour market status, work conditions, and self-reported health, allowing analysis of how working conditions relate to health outcomes. Many of the specified countries participate, and the fieldwork took place in 2012 (within the 2010s)."},{"title":"EUROPEAN SOCIAL SURVEY 2014 (Round 7)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12253","justification":"ESS Round 7 (2014) again covers a broad set of European countries (including many listed) and contains repeated core items on employment, working hours, job demands and control, and self-reported health. These variables allow analysis of working conditions and occupational health during the 2010s in the requested countries."}]}
